prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print style. A lone samurai on a crowded wooden bridge, completely still, eyes fixed on one distant figure who is rendered in sharp flat colors. All other travelers on the bridge are drawn lightly, fading into the background. Katsushika Hokusai compositional style — strong diagonal bridge, Mount Fuji visible in distance. Flat unmodulated areas of color — indigo, vermillion, ivory, black. Bold visible outlines. No photographic shading or gradients. Woodgrain texture visible. No text, no labels, no diagrams.
tradition_approach: Targeted Hokusai-style diagonal composition, flat unmodulated color areas in indigo and vermillion, bold black outlines, visible woodgrain texture, and stark figure-ground contrast with no photographic shading.
